---
title: "Audit Story"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: false
    smooth-scroll: true
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    css: audit_story.css
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<!-- AUTO:COVER START -->
::: {.audit-card}
# Procurement Process Audit

**Company:** Lanninster-Soprano Joint Company (LSJC)<br>
**Audit period:** 2026-01-01 — 2026-04-01<br>
**Audit ID:** 2026-001
:::
<!-- AUTO:COVER END -->

# One-page audit story

<!-- AUTO:ONE_PAGE_STORY START -->
| Item | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Audit | Procurement Process Audit |
| Program rows | 4 |
| Observations | 3 |

::: {.manual-block}
Add a short narrative explaining what changed from initial plan to final conclusion.
:::
<!-- AUTO:ONE_PAGE_STORY END -->

# What we started with

<!-- AUTO:INITIAL_ASSUMPTIONS START -->
| Risk ID | Title | Initial rationale | Preliminary rating |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| R-001 | Unauthorized purchases | Purchases may be created, approved, or released without proper authority.  This risk is relevant because purchasing is decentralized and recent ERP workflow changes may have affected approval discipline. | high |
| R-002 | Duplicate payments | Invoices may be paid more than once due to duplicate invoice records, weak matching controls, manual payment processing, or inconsistent supplier master data. | medium |
| R-003 | Split purchases below approval threshold | Purchases may be split into smaller transactions to avoid higher approval thresholds. This risk is relevant where approval limits are based on transaction amount and purchasing is decentralized. | medium |
| R-004 | Incomplete goods receipt before invoice payment | Previous audits revealed several issues regarding payments for incomplete goods. We need to check controls and assess the risk | medium |
<!-- AUTO:INITIAL_ASSUMPTIONS END -->

# What changed during planning and why

<!-- AUTO:PLANNING_DECISIONS START -->
## Included risks

| Risk ID | Title | Rationale | Planned response |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| R-001 | Unauthorized purchases |  |  |
| R-002 | Duplicate payments |  |  |
| R-003 | Split purchases below approval threshold |  |  |



## Excluded risks

| Risk ID | Title | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- |
| R-004 | Incomplete goods receipt before invoice payment |  |
<!-- AUTO:PLANNING_DECISIONS END -->

# Scope and risk evolution

<!-- AUTO:SCOPE_RISK_EVOLUTION START -->
## Final in-scope areas
- Purchase order approval
- Invoice matching
- Payment approval

## Final out-of-scope areas
- Goods receipt
- Supplier master data changes relevant to payments
- Strategic sourcing
- Supplier performance management
<!-- AUTO:SCOPE_RISK_EVOLUTION END -->

# What we tested and what we concluded

<!-- AUTO:TESTING_TRACEABILITY START -->
| Test ID | Risk | Control | Hypothesis | Workpaper |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| T-001 | R-001 | C-001 | Some purchase orders may have been released before the required approval was completed. | WP-C-001 |
| T-002 | R-001 | C-002 | Some purchase orders may have been approved by users whose approval authority was below the purchase order amount. | WP-C-002 |
| T-003 | R-002 | C-003 | Duplicate or potentially duplicate invoices/payments may have been recorded because the duplicate review control is weakly designed. | WP-C-003 |
| T-004 | R-003 |  | Purchases may have been split into several smaller purchase orders to stay below approval thresholds. | WP-R-003-NO-CONTROL |
<!-- AUTO:TESTING_TRACEABILITY END -->

# Observations and final outcome

<!-- AUTO:OBSERVATIONS_OUTCOME START -->
| ID | Title | Severity | Status | Workpaper |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OBS-001 | Purchase orders released before required approval | High | draft | WP-C-001 |
| OBS-002 | Weak duplicate invoice and payment review | Medium | draft | WP-C-003 |
| OBS-003 | No formal monitoring of split purchases below approval thresholds | Medium | draft | WP-R-003-NO-CONTROL |
<!-- AUTO:OBSERVATIONS_OUTCOME END -->

# Evidence and document map

<!-- AUTO:EVIDENCE_MAP START -->
| File | Notes |
| --- | --- |
| 04_evidence\01_regulations\procurement_process_regulation.md |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\approval_matrix.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\approval_workflow.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\goods_receipts.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\invoices.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\payments.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\purchase_orders.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\scripts\__pycache__\procurement_analysis.cpython-311.pyc |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\scripts\extract_procurement_data.sql |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\scripts\procurement_analysis.py |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\suppliers.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\02_raw_data\users.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\03_correspondence\candidate_review_dispositions.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\03_correspondence\procurement_follow_up_summary.md |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\planning_dataset_overview.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\planning_date_ranges.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\planning_linkage_checks.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\planning_po_volume_by_month.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\t001_release_before_approval_candidates.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\t001_release_before_approval_review.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\t002_approval_authority_exceptions.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\t003_duplicate_invoice_candidate_records.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\t003_duplicate_invoice_groups.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\t003_duplicate_payment_review.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\t003_repeated_payment_amount_groups.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\t004_split_purchase_candidates.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\99_generated\t004_split_purchase_review.csv |  |
| 04_evidence\evidence_manifest.yml |  |
<!-- AUTO:EVIDENCE_MAP END -->

# Lessons learned

<!-- MANUAL:LESSONS_LEARNED START -->
::: {.manual-block}
**Manual section.** Add lessons learned after the engagement is complete.

Suggested prompts:

- Which planning assumptions turned out to be wrong?
- Which analytics or scripts should be reused in the next audit?
- Which data limitations should be resolved before future monitoring?
- What would the team do differently next time?
:::
<!-- MANUAL:LESSONS_LEARNED END -->
